LIB-1.symbol: In a collection of perfect specimens of many biological forms, a butterfly displays the beauty of its wings, its body impaled by a fine dart
LIB-1.keynote: The immortal archetypal reality that a perfect and dedicated life reveals.

LIB-2.symbol: The transmutation of the fruits of past experiences into the seed-realizations of the forever creative spirit
LIB-2.keynote: A repolarization of inner energies leading to a creative centralization of consciousness.

LIB-3.symbol: The dawn of a new day reveals everything changed
LIB-3.keynote: The ever-present possibility of beginning again on a new foundation of values.

LIB-4.symbol: A group of young people sit around a campfire in spiritual communion
LIB-4.keynote: The necessity to unite with kindred spirits as one enters a difficult path which one must tread alone.

LIB-5.symbol: A man revealing to his students the foundation of an inner knowledge upon which a new world could be built
LIB-5.keynote: The necessity for the youthful spirit to learn from the Teacher who through long experience has been able to reach concrete and communicable truths.

LIB-6.symbol: A man watches his ideals taking a concrete form before his inner vision
LIB-6.keynote: The inevitability of the flowering of the heart's fondest dreams.

LIB-7.symbol: A woman feeding chickens and protecting them from the hawks
LIB-7.keynote: The need to face the antagonism of dark powers as one attempts to feed the mind of a helpless and frightened humanity.

LIB-8.symbol: A blazing fireplace in a deserted home
LIB-8.keynote: The constant presence of unseen, sustaining agencies in every creation of man.

LIB-9.symbol: Three old masters hanging on the wall of a special room in an art gallery
LIB-9.keynote: The need to return to source during a confused search for new value in a chaotic society.

LIB-10.symbol: Having passed safely through narrow rapids, a canoe reaches calm waters
LIB-10.keynote: The self-control and poise necessary to reach a state of inner stability.

LIB-11.symbol: A professor peering over his glasses at his students
LIB-11.keynote: Problems attending the transmission of knowledge in a special cultural setup.

LIB-12.symbol: Miners are surfacing from a deep coal mine
LIB-12.keynote: The need to carry on at ever deeper levels the quest for knowledge which keeps the fires of the collective mind burning.

LIB-13.keynote: The cultural fantasies through which young minds dream of perfect fulfillment.

LIB-14.symbol: In the heat of the noon hour a man takes a siesta
LIB-14.keynote: The need for recuperation within the social pattern of everyday activity.

LIB-15.keynote: Coming to terms with the inevitability of establishing a steady rhythm of social activity.

LIB-16.symbol: After a storm a boat landing stands in need of reconstruction
LIB-16.keynote: The need to keep in operation steady links between the vast Unconscious and the ego-consciousness.

LIB-17.symbol: A retired sea captain watches ships entering and leaving the harbor
LIB-17.keynote: The capacity to gain an objective and calm understanding of human experiences in which one was once deeply involved.

LIB-18.keynote: The consequences of refusing to face the implications of a negative integration of the social values of one's environment.

LIB-19.symbol: A gang of robbers in hiding

LIB-20.keynote: The ability to draw on the power of an ancestral tradition in order to serve and inspire one's fellowmen.

LIB-21.symbol: A Sunday crowd enjoying the beach
LIB-21.keynote: A revivifying contact with the Mother-force of nature and of social togetherness.

LIB-22.symbol: A child giving birds a drink at a fountain
LIB-22.keynote: The solicitude of simple souls for the welfare and happiness of less-evolved beings who need help.

LIB-23.keynote: A creative and unsophisticated response to life.

LIB-24.symbol: A butterfly with a third wing on its left side
LIB-24.keynote: The ability to develop, for inner strengthening, new modes of response to basic life situations.

LIB-25.symbol: The sight of an autumn leaf brings to a pilgrim the sudden revelation of the mystery of life and death
LIB-25.keynote: The ability to discover in every experience a transcendent or cosmic meaning.

LIB-26.symbol: An eagle and a large white dove change into each other
LIB-26.keynote: The interaction of the spiritual Will and of the Love principle when critical needs arise.

LIB-27.symbol: An airplane sails, high in the clear sky
LIB-27.keynote: A consciousness able to transcend the conflicts and pressures of the personal life.

LIB-28.symbol: A man becoming aware of spiritual forces surrounding and assisting him
LIB-28.keynote: The realization, at any level of existence, that one is never alone, and that the community - visible or invisible - is sustaining one's efforts.

LIB-29.symbol: Mankind's vast and enduring effort to reach for knowledge transferrable from generation to generation
LIB-29.keynote: A deep sense of participation in, and commitment to, social processes which seek to bring to all men Truth and a greater Life.

LIB-30.symbol: Three mounds of knowledge on a philosopher's head
LIB-30.keynote: The fulfillment of man's power of understanding at whatever level of existence the person operates.

SCO-1.symbol: A crowded sightseeing bus on a city street
SCO-1.keynote: The fundamental human eagerness to expand one's social horizon and to experience the results of collective achievements and ways of life.

SCO-2.symbol: A delicate bottle of perfume lies broken, releasing its fragrance
SCO-2.keynote: The accidental nature of opportunities that impel one to break away from a past and not yet understood level of existence.

SCO-3.symbol: A house-raising party in a small village enlists the neighbors' cooperation
SCO-3.keynote: The feeling of community demonstrated in a basic collective effort.

SCO-4.symbol: A youth carries a lighted candle in a devotional ritual
SCO-4.keynote: The educative power of ceremonies which impress the great images of a culture upon its gathered participants.

SCO-5.symbol: A massive rocky shore resists the pounding of the sea
SCO-5.keynote: The inertia of all institutionalized procedures.

SCO-6.symbol: The gold rush tears men away from their native soil
SCO-6.keynote: The passionate search for new values which, at any level, promise a more abundant life.

SCO-7.keynote: The will to explore the hidden depths of all existence and to search for primordial causes.

SCO-8.symbol: A calm lake bathed in moonlight
SCO-8.keynote: A quiet openness to higher inspiration.

SCO-9.keynote: Overcoming the negative results of social practices and an ego-craving for softness.

SCO-10.symbol: A fellowship supper reunites old comrades
SCO-10.keynote: The overtones of human relationships based on a community of work or experiences.

SCO-11.symbol: A drowning man is being rescued
SCO-11.keynote: The deep concern of the social group for the safety of the individual.

SCO-12.keynote: Group-consciousness at the level of the highest hierarchy in cultural elaborations.

SCO-13.symbol: An inventor performs a laboratory experiment
SCO-13.keynote: The driving urge toward achievement, which is the basis of civilization.

SCO-14.symbol: Telephone linemen at work installing new connections
SCO-14.keynote: The need to establish new channels of communication.

SCO-15.symbol: Children playing around five mounds of sand
SCO-15.keynote: Early steps in the development of a mind seeking to be attuned to the higher level of human evolution.

SCO-16.symbol: A girl's face breaking into a smile
SCO-16.keynote: The fervent reach to extend one's being in new ways.

SCO-17.symbol: A woman, fecundated by her own spirit, is great with child
SCO-17.keynote: A total reliance upon the dictates of the God-within.

SCO-18.symbol: A path through woods rich in autumn coloring
SCO-18.keynote: The light which transfigures the sodden entities of the past: the light of fulfillment.

SCO-19.symbol: A parrot repeats the conversation he has overheard
SCO-19.keynote: The capacity to transmit transcendental knowledge.

SCO-20.symbol: A woman drawing aside two dark curtains that closed the entrance to a sacred pathway
SCO-20.keynote: The revelation to the human consciousness of what lies beyond dualistic knowledge.

SCO-21.symbol: Obeying his conscience, a soldier resists orders
SCO-21.keynote: A readiness to face the results of a refusal to follow the authoritarian patterns of an aggressive society.

SCO-22.keynote: The aggressive assertion of the individual or the collective will against nature.

SCO-23.symbol: A rabbit metamorphoses into a nature spirit
SCO-23.keynote: The raising of animal drives to a higher level.

SCO-24.symbol: After having heard an inspired individual deliver his sermon on the mount, crowds are returning home
SCO-24.keynote: The need to incorporate inspiring experiences and teachings into everyday living.

SCO-25.keynote: The capacity to acquire an understanding of the structural principles of reality.

SCO-26.symbol: American Indians making camp after moving into a new territory
SCO-26.keynote: The ability to adjust to a new situation by tuning in to its specific requirements.

SCO-27.symbol: A military band marches noisily on through the city streets
SCO-27.keynote: The aggressive glorification of cultural values.

SCO-28.symbol: The king of the fairies approaching his domain
SCO-28.keynote: The capacity to recognize and to pay homage to an integrating Principle at the core of all existence.

SCO-29.symbol: An Indian squaw pleading to the chief for the lives of her children
SCO-29.keynote: Love as a principle of redemption.

SCO-30.symbol: Children in Halloween costumes indulge in various pranks
SCO-30.keynote: The periodic release from social pressure.

SAG-1.symbol: Retired army veterans gather to reawaken old memories
SAG-1.keynote: The will to reaffirm the value of the struggle upon which civilization and group-achievements are founded.

SAG-2.symbol: White-capped waves display the power of wind over sea
SAG-2.keynote: The mobilization of unconscious energies under the pressure of superpersonal motives.

SAG-4.symbol: A little child learning to walk with the encouragement of his parents
SAG-4.keynote: The natural assistance of superior powers during crises of growth.

SAG-5.symbol: An old owl sits alone on the branch of a large tree
SAG-5.keynote: A poised and wise reliance upon traditional knowledge.

SAG-6.keynote: The solidarity required in the accumulation of group-prestige.

SAG-7.symbol: Cupid knocking at the door of a human heart
SAG-7.keynote: A stirring up of individual longings for romantic love.

SAG-8.symbol: Within the depths of the earth new elements are being formed
SAG-8.keynote: The alchemical fire which both purifies and transforms the very substance of man's inner life.

SAG-9.symbol: A mother leads her small child step by step up a steep stairway
SAG-9.keynote: The need in any social situation to assist the less evolved in their management of the problems which society forces upon all its members.

SAG-10.symbol: A theatrical representation of a golden-haired goddess of opportunity
SAG-10.keynote: Society's efforts at dramatizing the greatness of what it offers to the ambitious individual.

SAG-11.symbol: In the left section of an archaic temple, a lamp burns in a container shaped like a human body
SAG-11.keynote: The value of the return to the body advocated by modern thinkers in order to balance the stress on intellectuality and objective consciousness.

SAG-12.symbol: A flag turns into an eagle; the eagle into a chanticleer which salutes the dawn
SAG-12.keynote: The spiritualization and promotion of great symbols of a New Age by minds sensitive to its incipient manifestations.

SAG-13.symbol: A widow's past is brought to light
SAG-13.keynote: The karma of past actions as it affects the opportunities presented by a new cycle.

SAG-14.symbol: The Great Pyramid and the Sphinx
SAG-14.keynote: The enduring power of occult knowledge and of its quasi-divine Custodians, Seed-men of a previous cycle of existence.

SAG-15.symbol: The ground hog looking for its shadow on Ground-hog Day
SAG-15.keynote: The value of anticipating new turns of events and ascertaining future prospects.

SAG-16.symbol: Sea gulls fly around a ship in expectation of food
SAG-16.keynote: The dependence of psychic desires upon the stimulation of social circumstances.

SAG-17.symbol: An Easter sunrise service draws a large crowd
SAG-17.keynote: The culturally stimulated longing for group participation in a process of rebirth.

SAG-18.symbol: Children playing on the beach, their heads protected by sunbonnets
SAG-18.keynote: The protection society affords to as yet immature individuals as they begin to deal with the powerful energies of their unconscious nature.

SAG-19.symbol: Pelicans menaced by the behavior and refuse of men seek safer areas for bringing up their young
SAG-19.keynote: The need for people concerned with the future to discover a new way of living and more wholesome surroundings.

SAG-20.symbol: In winter people cutting ice from a frozen pond, for use in summer
SAG-20.keynote: The foresighted use of natural resources to supply future human needs.

SAG-21.symbol: A child and a dog wearing borrowed eyeglasses
SAG-21.keynote: The use of imagination and make-believe in anticipating higher stages of development.

SAG-22.keynote: Making use of the racial-cultural background in order to survive and prosper in an alien environment.

SAG-23.symbol: A group of immigrants as they fulfill the requirements of entrance into the new country
SAG-23.keynote: Conscious acceptance of the ways of a new stage of experience, in readiness for the opportunities it will present.

SAG-24.symbol: A bluebird perched on the gate of a cottage
SAG-24.keynote: The reward which meets every effort at integration into a social environment for the sake of self-development.

SAG-25.symbol: A chubby boy on a hobby-horse
SAG-25.keynote: The anticipatory enjoyment of powers one can only as yet dream of utilizing.

SAG-26.symbol: A flag bearer in a battle
SAG-26.keynote: The nobly accepted subservience of the individual to collective values and goals.

SAG-27.keynote: The ability to project one's vision upon and to give form to materials.

SAG-28.symbol: An old bridge over a beautiful stream is still in constant use
SAG-28.keynote: The enduring elements in a society which reveal its ability to significantly link the genius of its individuals to the everyday needs of the collectivity.

SAG-29.symbol: A fat boy mowing the lawn of his house on an elegant suburban street
SAG-29.keynote: The need to attend to social respectability to which one belongs.

SAG-30.keynote: Paying homage to the glory of collective achievement.
